origin.name: Pakistan
origin.death_registration: National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA) or local Union Council
origin.investigating_authority: Pakistan Police and government forensic pathologist
origin.security_note: Parts of Pakistan are subject to FCDO travel warnings. Deaths in conflict-affected or restricted areas add significant complexity. Check FCDO guidance for the specific area.
origin.embalming_note: Quality embalming available in Islamabad, Karachi, and Lahore. Limited in smaller cities.
origin.cremation_note: Cremation is effectively unavailable in Pakistan. All repatriations are full body.
origin.key_document: Pakistan death certificate (NADRA-issued) with certified English translation
origin.source: FCDO Pakistan travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Islamabad guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Pakistan death certificate (NADRA or Union Council issued)
special_rules: Death certificate is in Urdu. Certified English translation is mandatory.
No cremation in Pakistan. All repatriations are full body.
Deaths in conflict-affected or FCDO-restricted areas add significant complexity. Seek specialist advice immediately.
Quality embalming limited to major cities.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Pakistani documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district is notified. Cases involving deaths in high-risk areas may require additional coroner review.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. British High Commission Islamabad and consulates in Karachi and Lahore can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Pakistan to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Pakistan. Repatriation to the UK takes 3-6 weeks. Urdu documentation, direct flights from Islamabad. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
key_documents: Pakistan death certificate with certified English translation
special_rules: Irish Embassy in Islamabad handles consular matters.
Certified English translation required identically to UK repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All documentation must be in certified English. The Coroner is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Islamabad can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Pakistan to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Pakistan and needs to come home to Ireland. 3-6 weeks. Urdu documentation and all handled. Contact us 24/7.
